strategic_profile.executive_summary: Mercer University is a historic, private R2 research university in Georgia known for its heavy emphasis on community service, health sciences, engineering, and business. It offers a unique blend of a close-knit liberal arts college atmosphere with the academic resources and pre-professional programs of a larger research institution.

analyst_takeaways.insight: High placement rates in health sciences and engineering.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Ideal for pre-med, nursing, and engineering students looking for a supportive environment with strong clinical pipelines in Georgia.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Mercer on Mission program and historic faith-based roots heavily influence the campus culture.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants should highlight community service, leadership, and a desire to make a global impact in their essays.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Demonstrated interest is tracked and heavily weighed.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Prospective students must actively engage with admissions counselors and visit campus if possible to improve their odds.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Tight-knit community with a strong emphasis on service, a massive intramural sports program, and active Greek life. The campus is peaceful and beautiful, though some students express mixed feelings about the surrounding Macon area.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: The Atlanta campus is heavily commuter-friendly with access to MARTA, while the main Macon campus is a traditional residential environment.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Classified as an R2 institution with significant undergraduate research opportunities, particularly in rural health disparities and engineering.

current_status.test_policy_details: Test Optional. Students may apply without submitting standardized test scores, though competitive programs may evaluate submitted scores for merit scholarships.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist accepting spot data not reported publicly, but 116 students were eventually admitted from the waitlist.

gpa.notes: GPA reflects rigorous high school curriculum expectations.

testing.policy_note: 17% of admitted students submitted ACT scores; 32% submitted SAT scores.

colleges.name: Stetson-Hatcher School of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for career-oriented students seeking flexible business education with immediate opportunities to transition into an MBA via the Special Consideration Program.
colleges.housing_profile: Traditional dorms in Macon; commuter-friendly setting in Atlanta.
colleges.student_archetype: Collaborative, driven leaders with a focus on real-world application.

majors.name: General Business
majors.notes: Accounting courses are frequently cited by students as major weed-out classes for second- and third-year students.

colleges.name: School of Engineering
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Strong math and science students looking for rigorous engineering programs mixed with hands-on, service-oriented projects.

majors.name: Computer Engineering
majors.notes: Statics (EGR 232), Thermodynamics (EGR 235), and Dynamics (EGR 236) are widely considered intense weed-out courses. A supplemental instruction and retest program has been implemented to help retention.

colleges.name: College of Liberal Arts and Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students pursuing foundational arts and sciences degrees or planning to transfer into the highly competitive Nursing/Pharmacy schools later.

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.notes: Offers an ABET-accredited B.S. degree. The Computational Science offshoot pairs math and computer science.

application_deadlines.notes: Early Action I. Priority for merit scholarships; ideal for strong applicants.
Early Action II. Still competitive for aid.

supplemental_requirements.details: Real weight is placed on personal essays to determine a candidate's fit for Mercer's faith-based, service-oriented culture.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Admissions officers heavily prioritize course rigor and service-oriented extracurriculars that match their 'Mercer On Mission' philosophy.

major_selection_tactics: Demonstrate a clear fit for service-heavy programs.
If interested in graduate programs, leverage the Special Consideration Program (Double Bear) pathways.
college_ranking_tactics: Visit campus or attend virtual information sessions to maximize demonstrated interest tracking.
Reach out directly to your assigned regional admissions counselor with specific program questions.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Consider applying to the College of Liberal Arts and Sciences to fulfill prerequisites if direct admit to nursing or engineering seems too competitive, then transition internally.

scholarships.name: Stamps Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Covers full tuition, fees, housing, food, an Apple iPad, and a $16,000 enrichment stipend.

scholarships.name: Regents Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Full tuition waiver for 4 years plus a $4,000 annual stipend.

scholarships.name: Presidential Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Full tuition waiver for 2 years plus a $4,000 annual stipend.

ap_policy.general_rule: Awards credit based on score and course equivalent as determined by individual departments.
exceptions: No credit awarded for scores of 1 or 2.
Maximum of 32 hours allowed from all extra-course examinations combined (AP, IB, CLEP).

ib_policy.general_rule: Awards credit for scores of 5, 6, or 7 on Higher Level (HL) exams.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Maximum 64 semester credits accepted from 2-year colleges. A minimum of 32 semester credits must be completed in residence at Mercer.

what_it_takes: A high school GPA of 3.5 or above with rigorous course selection.
Clear, trackable demonstrated interest in the university.
common_activities: Tailgating for NCAA Division I FCS football
essay_tips: Highlight personal fit with Mercer's faith-based, service-oriented culture.
Explain your passion for your chosen field and how it contributes to society.
insights: The Mercer name carries significant weight across Georgia, especially in healthcare, business, and legal job opportunities.
The campus is known for its beauty, but some students report mixed feelings regarding limited food options and the surrounding area in Macon.
Small class sizes foster deep, supportive connections with professors.
